channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:summary: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations.

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceDaily] AI reveals the invisible magnetic chaos wasting energy inside electric motors and [Tom's Hardware] Texas county passes data center ban for rural areas for a year, move comes in wake of AI data centers moving to remote areas to skirt regulations — state senator says counties cannot legally impose these bans, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Does the Cariboo have the institutional capacity to adopt agricultural innovations and manage competing land uses, or is the regional governance infrastructure too thin to implement solutions regardless of technological readiness? Topics include: Perennial grain economics and the yield-penalty transition problem in Cariboo agriculture, Agricultural financing gaps and why lender structures prevent innovation adoption, Regional research capacity deficits and the mismatch between global research agendas and local agricultural needs, Data centre land use competition with agriculture and grid reliability impacts, Indigenous-led land stewardship as an institutional capacity model, The timing mismatch between technological readiness, institution-building, and external land use pressures.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Coast Tourism Association

Sources discussed:

  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] AI reveals the invisible magnetic chaos wasti...
  • Tom's Hardware: [Tom's Hardware] Texas county passes data center ban for rur...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] What 'cascading outages' near AI data centers mean ...
  • APTN News: [APTN News] Baffinland Iron Mines seeking creditor protectio...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Environmental advocates call pipelin...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Wild world record broken by barefoot...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] A Start-Up Aiming to Make Geothermal Energy M...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists just unlocked a cheaper way to mak...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] RS-485 Sprinkler Control: Scaling Irrigation Acro...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Solar power could finally be viable with this inven...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Forget electrons, this breakthrough uses ligh...
  • Tom's Hardware: [Tom's Hardware] SoftBank to manufacture its own batteries w...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] This US start-up wants to pay you to host a data...
  • InsideEVs: [InsideEVs] Congress Wants You To Pay $130 A Year Just To Dr...
  • Krebs on Security: [Krebs on Security] CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on G...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] My Classmate, ChatGPT
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Genetic discovery could change how rice is grown...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] US data centers use enough electricity to power ...
  • Tom's Hardware: [Tom's Hardware] OpenClaw creator burned through $1.3 millio...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceDaily] AI reveals the invisible magnetic chaos wasting energy inside electric motors and [Tom's Hardware] Texas county passes data center ban for rural areas for a year, move comes in wake of AI data centers moving to remote areas to skirt regulations — state senator says counties cannot legally impose these bans, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Does the Cariboo have the institutional capacity to adopt agricultural innovations and manage competing land uses, or is the regional governance infrastructure too thin to implement solutions regardless of technological readiness? Topics include: Perennial grain economics and the yield-penalty transition problem in Cariboo agriculture, Agricultural financing gaps and why lender structures prevent innovation adoption, Regional research capacity deficits and the mismatch between global research agendas and local agricultural needs, Data centre land use competition with agriculture and grid reliability impacts, Indigenous-led land stewardship as an institutional capacity model, The timing mismatch between technological readiness, institution-building, and external land use pressures.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Coast Tourism Association

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechCrunch] Wirestock raises $23M to supply creative multi-modal data to AI labs and [Open Culture] When the Nobel Prize Committee Rejected The Lord of the Rings: Tolkien “Has Not Measured Up to Storytelling of the Highest Quality” (1961), plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When, if ever, is AI-generated cultural content justified, and what governance structures and guardrails should protect communities from extraction while enabling legitimate cultural participation? Topics include: NPR study on arts engagement and biological aging at cellular level, AI content flooding discoverability landscapes and what it displaces versus what it doesn't, Indigenous sovereignty and community control over cultural production tools and governance, Project-based funding gaps and the failure of pilots to become sustainable infrastructure, Extraction models in tech (Wirestock, IP-as-infrastructure) versus community-serving design.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Partners for Literacy

Sources discussed:

  • TechCrunch: [TechCrunch] Wirestock raises $23M to supply creative multi-...
  • Open Culture: [Open Culture] When the Nobel Prize Committee Rejected The L...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Mixapps: The Mixtape of The Internet Age
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] We’re Only Human. And That’ll Do
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] MrBeast revealed a new membership program...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Lost 1,200-year-old manuscript contains the f...
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] Introducing our 2026 ReFocus photojournalism fe...
  • NPR Health News: [NPR Health News] Engaging with the arts can slow biological...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] “I cannot divorce the two”: How Star Wars is ble...
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] A new GyaaG̲ang (totem pole) is raised in Haida...

Additional sources provided:

  • Neowin: [Neowin] Spotify will soon support Apple's technology to ope...
  • The Marginalian: [The Marginalian] How to Keep Criticism from Sinking Your So...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Paramount+ is exploring adding podcasts, ...
  • The Verge: [The Verge] YouTube is courting creators — and sponsors — wi...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Heartbreak in Dawson City after hist...
  • Resilience.org: [Resilience.org] In the Rising Tide, Episode 5. Raviraj Shet...
  • Lifehacker: [Lifehacker] 10 Shows Like 'Paradise' You Should Watch Next
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] As Election Looms, Band Halts Chief’s Credit Card...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] What being a merch girl for Amy Winehouse and J...
  • The Atlantic Culture: [The Atlantic Culture] When Bots Write Comedy, the Joke’s on...
  • Engadget: [Engadget] Apple acquired another company that could help bu...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Join Us for Reality Check LIVE on May 21

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When, if ever, is AI-generated cultural content justified, and what governance structures and guardrails should protect communities from extraction while enabling legitimate cultural participation? Topics include: NPR study on arts engagement and biological aging at cellular level, AI content flooding discoverability landscapes and what it displaces versus what it doesn't, Indigenous sovereignty and community control over cultural production tools and governance, Project-based funding gaps and the failure of pilots to become sustainable infrastructure, Extraction models in tech (Wirestock, IP-as-infrastructure) versus community-serving design.

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceDaily] Stunning 150-million-year-old stegosaur skull rewrites dinosaur evolution and [ScienceDaily] Paleontology rocked by discovery of organic molecules in 66-million-year-old dinosaur bones, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can the Cariboo region develop locally-controlled, long-term scientific monitoring infrastructure using accessible methodologies without reproducing extractive research models? Topics include: Environmental DNA methodology for monitoring aquatic species without capture or disturbance, Indigenous data sovereignty and governance requirements for research conducted on traditional territories, Acoustic ecology and plant responses to environmental sound in forest regeneration contexts, Old-growth forests as scientific archives containing climate and cosmic event records, Community foundations and endowment models as sustainable funding vehicles for long-term ecological monitoring.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel Community Foundation

Sources discussed:

  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Stunning 150-million-year-old stegosaur skull...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Paleontology rocked by discovery of organic m...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] JWST discovers a galaxy that doesn’t spin in the...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists discover the Southern Ocean is “sw...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] The Earliest Known Dentistry Wasn't Done By O...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Crickets Respond to Injuries in a Way That Su...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] JWST Reveals a Hidden Structure in The Heart ...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] First-ever direct image of the cosmic web rev...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Perseverance Snaps a Selfie on Mars
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Newly Discovered Asteroid to Make Close Pass by E...
  • WIRED: [WIRED] Asteroid 2026 JH2 Is About to Fly Right Past Earth—R...

Additional sources provided:

  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] After 100 years, scientists finally uncover h...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists reversed memory loss by recharging...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Who are the Japanese? Huge DNA discovery rewr...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Surprise X-Ray Discovery May Explain Strange ...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Neanderthal dentists used stone drills to fix an...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] First Signatures of a Future Tectonic Split A...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Deadly “red sky” solar storm from 800 years a...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] NASA’s Hubble reveals a giant chaotic planet...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists put a tiny lump of metal in two pl...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Record-breaking volcanic eruption somehow cleane...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Stardust Trapped in Antarctic Ice Reveals Ear...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Quantum breakthrough could revolutionize tele...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Giant squid discovery uncovers a hidden deep-...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Earth is flying through ancient supernova deb...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Plants may literally hear the sound of approachi...

Closing Music: “Behind The Walls” by Non Camera — pop, rock, indie. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Cariboo Local Affairs
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Local Affairs.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [100 Mile Free Press] HANSEN: Lac La Hache Community Club president steps up to run bingo and [Williams Lake Tribune] 2025 Williams Lake Fire Department recruits earn Live Fire Interior Attack certification, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What combination of external recognition, cultural transmission, and civic investment creates sustainable rural community resilience, and how do communities choose what to anchor their identity to? Topics include: The biomass award as external validation of local problem-solving, Cultural transmission through youth fiddle society and its relationship to retention, Leadership succession and bench depth in volunteer organizations, High-infrastructure anchors (junior hockey) versus low-infrastructure sustainable activities, The distinction between sustaining activities and anchoring community identity.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Williams Lake Women's Centre

Sources discussed:

  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] HANSEN: Lac La Hache Community Club pr...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] 2025 Williams Lake Fire Department r...

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Traditional powwow honouring familie...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Delegation recognizes Laker’s Go Bus...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake’s Mud Puddle Trail Run...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Hands-on trades event inspires futur...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] FORSTER: $7,000 raised for Mountain Sp...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] 100 Mile Wranglers preparing for innag...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] 100 Mile House students preview future...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] $2M in funding to bring stability, c...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Province recommends grab-and-go kits...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] PHOTOS: Cariboo Chilcotin Youth Fidd...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Central Chilcotin Rehabilitation Ltd...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake fighters return from s...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] 100 Mile House rookie officer settles ...

Additional sources provided:

  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Quesnel schools’ leadership shake...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] FOREST OPERATIONS MAPs #3172 – IN...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Lone Butte’s OUT Group creates and sup...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Tŝilhqot’in National Government warn...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] UPDATE: B.C. Amber Alert out of Terr...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. Conservatives strip Independent...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Missing snowmobiler separated from g...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Significant burnout’: Vernon psychi...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Disappointed’: Revelstoke losing 12...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Palace Hotel first entry in new Clinto...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] PSO Plant Sale supports 100 Mile stude...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘We are exhausted’: Chilliwack man, ...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Motor vehicle accident leads to arrest...

Closing Music: “Handcrafted” by Fresh Body Shop — indie, pop. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Resilience.org] Environmental protection depends on more than regulation and [Williams Lake Tribune] Help B.C. communities recover from wildfires 1 beer at a time, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can technology tools and targeted institutional interventions meaningfully improve wildfire preparedness and emergency survival readiness in rural Cariboo communities? Topics include: Satellite communicator technology accessibility and pricing for rural populations, AI-assisted emergency planning tools and demographic limitations, Interoperability and standards gaps in emergency communication devices, Community social infrastructure versus top-down institutional programs, Role of local institutions (credit unions, search and rescue, Regional Districts) in building preparedness.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Scout Island Nature Centre

Sources discussed:

  • Resilience.org: [Resilience.org] Environmental protection depends on more th...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Help B.C. communities recover from w...
  • Ricochet: [Ricochet] This isn’t a natural disaster. It’s a policy fail...
  • Global News: [Global News] Higher water levels reported in central Saskat...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C., feds reach agreement to advanc...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Self-destructing $2,000 Nvidia chips will soon p...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Utah just approved a data center twice the size ...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] One of the biggest data center projects in the U...
  • CNET: [CNET] How to Use AI to Plan the Camping Trip You Really Wan...
  • The Verge: [The Verge] Garmin’s mini satellite communicators are on sal...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] UPDATE: Wildfire in Chasm Provincial...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. residents urged to prepare for ...

Additional sources provided:

  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] CARIBOO OUTDOORS: When the breakup fin...
  • Resilience.org: [Resilience.org] Rebuilding after wildfire: Paradise, Califo...
  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] A very good, very 2026 headline: Japan Runs Ou...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Cerebras' 68% day-one rally kicks off a y...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Poor sleep not just symptom, but potential sign ...
  • MakeUseOf: [MakeUseOf] Bitwarden is great, but I switched to this bette...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] The race to post-quantum cryptography is already be...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] One Habit May Protect You From Work Stress Mo...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] Cerebras, A.I. Chip Maker, Rises 89% in Marke...
  • MacRumors: [MacRumors] Apple Alerted to macOS Security Vulnerability Un...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] AI data centers are pushing past 1 gigawatt. Here's...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Not in my backyard': New Gallup data shows most...
  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Yellowstone grizzly introduces two hikers to t...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Resilience.org] Environmental protection depends on more than regulation and [Williams Lake Tribune] Help B.C. communities recover from wildfires 1 beer at a time, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can technology tools and targeted institutional interventions meaningfully improve wildfire preparedness and emergency survival readiness in rural Cariboo communities? Topics include: Satellite communicator technology accessibility and pricing for rural populations, AI-assisted emergency planning tools and demographic limitations, Interoperability and standards gaps in emergency communication devices, Community social infrastructure versus top-down institutional programs, Role of local institutions (credit unions, search and rescue, Regional Districts) in building preparedness.

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] BC’s New Chief Treaty Commissioner on the Path Forward and [Global News] Manitoba’s nursing regulator apologizes for discrimination against Indigenous Peoples, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can Indigenous communities build internal technical capacity to implement data sovereignty frameworks, and what is the role of long-term human capital development versus short-term institutional solutions? Topics include: Indspire scholarship pipeline and long-term capacity building, UVic digital collections project and governance authority over digitization, FirstVoices as model for community-controlled digital infrastructure, Institutional hiring practices and the difference between advisory roles and decision-making authority, Funding models and structural durability: government dependency versus charitable sector diversification.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Williams Lake Indian Band

Sources discussed:

  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] BC’s New Chief Treaty Commissioner on the Path Fo...
  • Global News: [Global News] Manitoba’s nursing regulator apologizes for di...
  • APTN News: [APTN News] UN advice raises pressure on Liberals to end Ind...
  • Global News: [Global News] Mikisew Cree First Nation sues Alberta, feds o...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] 4 wind projects selected from BC Hyd...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. announces up to 5,000 new trade...
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Digital pathways into the University Art Collections - Libra...

Additional sources provided:

  • The Guardian Global Development: [The Guardian Global Development] Official marking of land f...
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] IndigiNews is now a registered charity!
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Victoria nurses lead greener approac...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] New AI data centres proposed for Van...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Tesla has a possible fix for chaotic char...
  • The Verge: [The Verge] The border is everywhere
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Training people in B.C. for in-demand skilled ...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] Honda Posts First Ever Annual Loss After Pull...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] Silicon Valley’s A.I. Lobbying Blitz Reach...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] The FBI just remotely reset thousands of home an...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Anthropic CFO says AI now writes 90% of i...
  • WIRED: [WIRED] What It Will Take to Make AI Sustainable
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Soaring from coast to coast for Indspire
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Soaring from coast to coast for Indspire

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] BC’s New Chief Treaty Commissioner on the Path Forward and [Global News] Manitoba’s nursing regulator apologizes for discrimination against Indigenous Peoples, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can Indigenous communities build internal technical capacity to implement data sovereignty frameworks, and what is the role of long-term human capital development versus short-term institutional solutions? Topics include: Indspire scholarship pipeline and long-term capacity building, UVic digital collections project and governance authority over digitization, FirstVoices as model for community-controlled digital infrastructure, Institutional hiring practices and the difference between advisory roles and decision-making authority, Funding models and structural durability: government dependency versus charitable sector diversification.

item.title: Gear, Gadgets And Practical Tech
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Tom's Hardware] Louis Rossmann tells 3D printer maker Bambu Lab to ‘Go (Bleep) yourself’ over its threatened lawsuit against enthusiast — Right to Repair advocate offers to pay the legal fees for a threatened OrcaSlicer developer and [Hackaday] Trying to Fix a GoPro Hero 10 With No Camera Input Issue, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities build sustainable, maintainable technology infrastructure when manufacturers increasingly restrict ownership rights and technical expertise is geographically concentrated? Topics include: Bambu Lab lawsuit and proprietary ecosystem lock-in versus right-to-repair, Hybrid DIY/commercial manufacturing models for complex systems, Rural technology equity and geographic access barriers, Federal and provincial right-to-repair legislation landscape, Tool-lending libraries and institutional infrastructure for rural tech access.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Regional District Library

Sources discussed:

  • Tom's Hardware: [Tom's Hardware] Louis Rossmann tells 3D printer maker Bambu...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Trying to Fix a GoPro Hero 10 With No Camera Inpu...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I saved $800 on an anamorphic lens by 3D pr...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] How To Avoid Failed Screw Holes In 3D Printed Par...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Direct FDM Printing with Granules
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] The Noctua Fan Files and the Limits of 3D Printin...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Win95-Tracker-CYD is a Cheap Yellow Mod Tracker w...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I ran three home server apps on the Orange ...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] I jailbroke my old Kindle to install KOReader
  • Tom's Hardware: [Tom's Hardware] $200 'socketed' Nvidia AI GPU for servers h...
  • WIRED: [WIRED] The Unitree GD01 Is a Giant Mecha Robot You Can Actu...

Additional sources provided:

  • Tom's Hardware: [Tom's Hardware] 3DMakerPro Toucan 3D Scanner review: All-in...
  • CNET: [CNET] This 4-Tool Head 3D Printer Is So Much Fun I Gave It ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Multimaterial SLA Printer Will Make Your Head Spi...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] 3D Printed Train Whistles Sound Out at Full Scale
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I built the weirdest possible NAS from hard...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Why I built my external SSD instead of buying ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Getting a Proprietary-Bus GPU onto PCIe Enables C...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] A $30 USB drive just saved me from a $500 PC r...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I turned my old laptop into a retro gaming ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] The Montgomery Ward Gasoline-Powered Clothes Iron
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Mermaid Clutch-Purse Cyberdeck is Unappologetical...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] You're wrecking your Wi-Fi speeds by ignori...
  • Newsletter: m***@make.co: Skiing on Jet Power
  • Newsletter: m***@make.co: Skiing on Jet Power
  • Newsletter: m***@make.co: Skiing on Jet Power

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechRadar] Fuelled by AI, Micron's $50 billion chip factory in Idaho will use billions of litres of water every year — but the US tech giant stays silent on where that new water will come from and [TechRadar] OpenAI's ‘Trusted Contact’ feature for ChatGPT users in crisis is a sign AI is becoming something much more personal, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can BC deploy AI-driven mineral exploration technology responsibly, or must governance capacity be built before the technology accelerates discovery? Topics include: AI mineral exploration technology and KoBold Metals' competitive advantages, Institutional capacity gaps in provincial and Indigenous consultation and assessment processes, Data sovereignty and public value capture from publicly-funded geological data, Nation-level technical capacity building as a structural intervention in resource governance, Sequencing of technology deployment versus governance system readiness.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Williams Lake Food Policy Council

Sources discussed:

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Fuelled by AI, Micron's $50 billion chip factory...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] OpenAI's ‘Trusted Contact’ feature for ChatGPT u...
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Forging powerful partnerships to compete in gl...
  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Safeway blames a "wood pulp shortage" for its ...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] USDA is giving California peach farmers $9 million ...

Additional sources provided:

  • InsideEVs: [InsideEVs] While You Stop For A 30-Minute Charge, Nio Just ...
  • NPR Health News: [NPR Health News] Forest therapy: Why a physician wants more...
  • InsideEVs: [InsideEVs] BMW Has Built Way More EVs Than You Think
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] A $340,000 bonus isn't enough — Samsung workers...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] Inside Haida Gwaii’s historic plan to ditch di...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] A logistics company uses AI to reduce its...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Common Sweetener May Harm Critical Brain Barr...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I replaced ChatGPT with a local model on my...
  • Engadget: [Engadget] Google announces its first-ever discovery of a ze...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] ‘Dizziness, nausea, vertigo, and sleep disruptio...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] RCR announces new lifts for Fernie, ...
  • TechCrunch: [TechCrunch] Thinking Machines wants to build an AI that act...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Startup harnesses the power of AI in the ongoing...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] $12M coming from province to support...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt

Topics include: AI mineral exploration technology and KoBold Metals' competitive advantages, Institutional capacity gaps in provincial and Indigenous consultation and assessment processes, Data sovereignty and public value capture from publicly-funded geological data, Nation-level technical capacity building as a structural intervention in resource governance, Sequencing of technology deployment versus governance system readiness.

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [NYT Business] James Murdoch’s Company Said to Be in Talks to Acquire Major Parts of Vox Media and [Engadget] Spotify now lets AI agents like OpenClaw generate personal podcasts, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural cultural institutions build sustainable, discoverable storytelling capacity in an environment flooded with AI-generated content while maintaining community control and authentic relationships? Topics include: AI-generated content's impact on discoverability for authentic rural cultural storytelling, Governance models balancing community control with visibility (Mukurtu tiered-access system), Funding models for digital capacity at small cultural institutions (provincial arts funding vs. co-op models), Technology's relationship to community vitality (downstream not upstream), Place-specific authenticity as defense against synthetic content and media pressure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Museum of the Cariboo Chilcotin

Sources discussed:

  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] James Murdoch’s Company Said to Be in Talks t...
  • Engadget: [Engadget] Spotify now lets AI agents like OpenClaw generate...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Podslop' is a real and growing problem — data s...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I walked away from running a successful s...
  • WIRED: [WIRED] This Reggae Band Is in a Nightmare Battle Against AI...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] A Very American Controversy at the Venicle...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] Justin Wolfers, Cable’s Favorite Economist, J...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] New art installation unveiled at Wil...
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] Winnipeg exhibition traces the revival of Red R...

Additional sources provided:

item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [NYT Business] James Murdoch’s Company Said to Be in Talks to Acquire Major Parts of Vox Media and [Engadget] Spotify now lets AI agents like OpenClaw generate personal podcasts, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural cultural institutions build sustainable, discoverable storytelling capacity in an environment flooded with AI-generated content while maintaining community control and authentic relationships? Topics include: AI-generated content's impact on discoverability for authentic rural cultural storytelling, Governance models balancing community control with visibility (Mukurtu tiered-access system), Funding models for digital capacity at small cultural institutions (provincial arts funding vs. co-op models), Technology's relationship to community vitality (downstream not upstream), Place-specific authenticity as defense against synthetic content and media pressure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Museum of the Cariboo Chilcotin

Sources discussed:

  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] James Murdoch’s Company Said to Be in Talks t...
  • Engadget: [Engadget] Spotify now lets AI agents like OpenClaw generate...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Podslop' is a real and growing problem — data s...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I walked away from running a successful s...
  • WIRED: [WIRED] This Reggae Band Is in a Nightmare Battle Against AI...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] A Very American Controversy at the Venicle...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] Justin Wolfers, Cable’s Favorite Economist, J...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] New art installation unveiled at Wil...
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] Winnipeg exhibition traces the revival of Red R...

  • Engadget: [Engadget] A new Subnautica 2 gameplay trailer just dropped ...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] I stopped treating ChatGPT like Google — and ev...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Debian's next release just made it near-imp...

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [NYT Top Stories] New Study Shows Risks of Amazon Deforestation. And Rewards of Protection. and [ScienceDaily] Scientists make stunning discovery that could change our understanding of the Universe, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can recent scientific discoveries about conserved mechanisms in regeneration, evolution, and quantum systems translate into actionable improvements for rural communities like the Cariboo? Topics include: Gene therapy and regenerative medicine accessibility in rural communities, Evolutionary conservation of genetic expression mechanisms and ecological succession prediction, Indigenous data sovereignty and co-investigator research partnerships with universities, Monitoring commons governance and the role of temporal sequence in system dynamics, Analytical capacity as the primary bottleneck rather than data availability or discovery.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Habitat for Humanity Cariboo

Sources discussed:

  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] New Study Shows Risks of Amazon Deforestat...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists make stunning discovery that could...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Earthquake 'whiplash' seen in large seismic even...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Physicists just found a tiny flaw in time its...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Ice age humans in China crafted surprisingly ...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Putting the whole Earth into a computer’: Chine...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] MIT scientists discover millions of “silent s...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Evolution isn’t random. Scientists find the s...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists just created exotic new forms of m...

Additional sources provided:

  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] What scientists found inside coral reefs coul...
  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] Animated Artemis II Photos Reveal Satellites Bu...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Movement Triggers a Hidden 'Brain Cleaning' M...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Antarctica is melting from below and scientis...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists stunned as volcano cloud destroys ...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Curiosity Drilled Into a Rock on Mars And Gav...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Elusive Quantum Material Turns Out to Be Some...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Scientists Found An 'Impossible' Atmosphere o...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] We Now Know When Dog Brains Shrank – And It M...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Physicists discover quantum particles that br...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Webb space telescope reveals a scorching “sup...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Look Up! The Halley's Comet Meteor Shower Is ...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Scientists found the “holy grail” gene that c...

Closing Music: “Runaways” by Elle Lefant — indie, electronic, pop. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
